president.name: Seth Bodnar (announced step-down Jan 2026; transition underway)

ai_strategy_lead.name: Amy Ratto Parks, Michael Cassens, Zach Rossmiller (tri-lead)
ai_strategy_lead.title: Future Project co-leads (Associate Director of Academic Assessment and Faculty Support; Professor of Media Arts; CIO)

documents.title: UM's Approach to Artificial Intelligence (President's statement)
The Future Project — Teaching, Learning and Work with Artificial Intelligence

ai_strategy.summary: UM's signature AI strategy is 'The Future Project,' a three-phase university-wide initiative launched in spring 2025 with philanthropic support from Montana-based consultancy Craton. It is organized around four lines of effort — How We Teach, What We Teach, How We Work, and Industry Demands — and is coordinated by 55+ cross-campus coordinators. President Seth Bodnar's 2025 public statement commits UM to pursue AI with 'integrity, urgency, and care,' anchored in critical inquiry, transparency, human dignity, academic freedom, and equitable access. The strategy frames UM's approach as deliberately human-centered and values-based rather than rapid-adoption, culminating in a Center for Human Centered AI and a draft AI Commitments document.

key_provisions: Instructors required to include a GenAI statement in every course syllabus clarifying whether AI use is welcomed, limited, or discouraged
Faculty are permitted to set their own classroom AI policies; no campus-wide prohibition
Prohibits inputting FERPA-protected student records, HR records, health information, or human-subject research data into unapproved AI tools
Faculty directed NOT to use AI detection tools (no enterprise contract; high false-positive rates for multilingual and neurodivergent writers)
Student Code of Conduct plagiarism definition updated to encompass generative AI
Accessibility and equity considerations: avoid assignments that require students to purchase premium AI tools
Transparency statements encouraged when faculty use AI in course design
ai_policy.summary: UM's posture is permissive and faculty-led: there is no campus-wide prohibition, but every syllabus must carry a GenAI policy statement, and faculty may choose from five example stances ranging from 'collaborative exploration encouraged' to 'use discouraged.' The Internal Audit unit has published specific data-privacy guidance forbidding unapproved-AI use with FERPA, HR, health, or human-subject data, and the Student Code of Conduct was updated to treat undisclosed AI use under plagiarism.

bodies.name: Future Project Coordinator Team
bodies.scope: 55+ faculty/staff coordinators organized under four lines of effort (How We Teach, What We Teach, How We Work, Industry Demands); delivered recommendations to the President in May 2025

bodies.name: Center for Human Centered AI (planned)
bodies.scope: Interdisciplinary hub connecting education, business, computer science, humanities, health, and creative arts faculty around shared AI integration approaches; approved by Board of Regents in November 2025

bodies.name: Cross-departmental AI Working Groups
bodies.scope: Formed March 2024 to coordinate AI exploration across units; feed quarterly community surveys and usage analysis

bodies.name: MUS AI Task Force
bodies.scope: Montana University System-wide task force coordinating AI guidance across MUS campuses; reviews and gives feedback on UM's AI approach

bodies.name: Office of Organizational Learning and Development (OOLD)
bodies.scope: Hosts faculty AI inquiry projects, professional development, and AI-focused event series across 2025–2026

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed rather than centralized: the Future Project tri-lead team (Ratto Parks / Cassens / Rossmiller) channels recommendations to the President, and a Center for Human Centered AI was approved by the Montana Board of Regents in Nov 2025 to become the coordinating hub. The MUS AI Task Force provides system-level coordination and reviews UM's approach.

initiatives.title: The Future Project — university-wide AI strategy initiative
initiatives.description: Three-phase, four-line-of-effort initiative launched spring 2025 with philanthropic funding from Craton. Over 55 coordinators produced recommendations to President Bodnar in May 2025; implementation is underway in Fall 2025 with pilot programs, community dialogue, and a forthcoming AI Commitments document.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a deliberately community-driven, values-based alternative to rapid AI adoption — organized institutional sense-making at scale, recognized by the 2026 CIO 100 Award.

investment.description: Philanthropic gift from Craton funded coordinator stipends, symposium, and platform piloting; specific dollar amount not publicly disclosed.

collaborations.description: Craton provided launch support and ongoing advisory to the Future Project.

initiatives.policy_implications: Produces the AI Commitments framework that will guide UM's enterprise policy, tool adoption, and curriculum decisions.

initiatives.title: Amplify GenAI — institutional generative AI platform
initiatives.description: UM is piloting Amplify GenAI, an institutional platform accessible at chat.umontana.ai that brokers access to leading frontier models (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude). Pilot began July 2025 with 75 users; intended to ensure equitable campus access to quality AI tools without individual license costs.
initiatives.significance: Institution-brokered multi-model access is a fast-emerging pattern for campuses that want to bypass per-user vendor fees and protect institutional data.

investment.funding_source: Institutional IT budget; open-source Amplify platform hosted by UM
investment.description: Pilot-phase deployment; final costs tied to model API usage and hosting.

collaborations.description: Frontier-model APIs are consumed through the Amplify gateway.

initiatives.policy_implications: Centralizes an approved, data-governed route for GenAI use, reducing risk from shadow-IT consumer tools.

initiatives.title: ChatMT — statewide MUS AI platform operated by UM
initiatives.description: ChatMT.ai is a secure, shared AI platform that gives every MUS student, faculty, and staff member access to leading Anthropic, OpenAI, and Meta foundation models through AWS Bedrock and LibreChat. Managed operationally by UM on behalf of the Montana University System, with institutional data sovereignty and a no-training guarantee.
initiatives.significance: Rare example of a single R1 operationalizing a multi-campus, multi-model AI gateway for an entire state university system.

investment.funding_source: Enterprise agreement with AWS Bedrock, under UM
investment.description: Centralized infrastructure intended to reduce per-campus costs for the whole MUS.

collaborations.description: Single platform spanning the MUS system.
AWS Bedrock provides foundation-model hosting; LibreChat the UI.

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes a sanctioned, data-sovereign alternative to consumer AI; enables consistent MUS-wide data governance.

initiatives.title: Center for Human Centered AI (approved by Board of Regents)
initiatives.description: Preliminary plan for an interdisciplinary center connecting faculty and staff across education, business, computer science, humanities, health, and creative arts. Approved by the Montana Board of Regents in November 2025; requires no additional funding and draws on existing capacity.
initiatives.significance: Signals institutional commitment to 'human-centered' AI as an organizing research and pedagogy agenda rather than a narrow CS program.

investment.funding_source: Existing institutional capacity (no new funding requested)
investment.description: Proposal expressly requires no additional funding; operates on existing faculty expertise and infrastructure.

collaborations.description: Board of Regents approved preliminary plans from both UM and MSU to stand up AI institutes.

initiatives.policy_implications: Positions 'human-centered AI' as UM's academic brand and governance lens.

initiatives.title: UM Flagship Fund AI track — internal seed grants
initiatives.description: The 2024 UM Flagship Fund offered one-year grants up to $10,000 to 'build the University's internal capacity to better utilize generative artificial intelligence.' Supported projects include a cross-disciplinary student/faculty project exploring AI-assisted learning across Biology and Computer Science.
initiatives.significance: Small-dollar seed funding is an efficient way to surface grassroots AI experimentation and build faculty confidence.

investment.description: Up to $10,000 per one-year project; multiple awards in 2024.

collaborations.description: Internal cross-disciplinary teams (e.g., Computer Science + Biology)

initiatives.title: ChatGPT EDU enterprise access campus-wide
initiatives.description: UM rolled out ChatGPT EDU access campus-wide in May 2025 alongside an OpenAI Academy pilot with 40 selected users, providing a managed pathway for students, faculty, and staff to use ChatGPT under enterprise terms.
initiatives.significance: Campus-wide ChatGPT EDU remains a leading signal of enterprise-scale generative-AI adoption in higher ed.

investment.description: Campus-wide ChatGPT EDU licenses plus OpenAI Academy training pilot.

collaborations.description: ChatGPT EDU enterprise agreement and OpenAI Academy training pilot.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates an enterprise channel where data handling is governed and non-training is guaranteed.

initiatives.title: SMART FIRES NSF EPSCoR project (AI/ML research thrust)
initiatives.description: Five-year, $20M NSF EPSCoR Track-1 award integrating Montana State's optical-sensor expertise with the University of Montana's environmental-sciences leadership and three tribal/community colleges. One of four thematic teams is Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, building decision-support tools for prescribed-burn and wildfire management.
initiatives.significance: Largest known federal AI-research anchor for UM; ties AI explicitly to workforce development for Montana's tribal and rural communities.

investment.description: $20M five-year EPSCoR Track-1 award across Montana institutions.

collaborations.description: Statewide six-institution consortium including tribal and community colleges.

initiatives.title: Oracle Cloud partnership — AI-accelerated weather forecasting
initiatives.description: UM's Autonomous Aerial Systems Office (AASO) partners with Oracle for Research to run the Weather Research & Forecast (WRF) model on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, achieving 60% faster runs than on in-house supercomputing. Project incorporates AI preconditioners for Arctic and Pacific Northwest operational planning.
initiatives.significance: Concrete example of AI-accelerated scientific workflows running on hyperscaler cloud infrastructure for a mid-size R1.

investment.funding_source: Oracle for Research cloud credits + UM research budgets
investment.description: Oracle Cloud credits, plus cloud-architect expertise in HPC, networking, and containers.

collaborations.description: Oracle provides OCI HPC infrastructure and solution architects.

initiatives.title: PromptMind — advanced prompt-engineering training platform
initiatives.description: UM launched PromptMind in September 2025 as an advanced training platform for prompt engineering, tied to the umontana.ai clearinghouse and the Future Project implementation phase.
initiatives.significance: Moves AI literacy beyond basic tool access to structured skill development, closing the 'uses ChatGPT / uses it well' gap.

investment.description: Platform subscription and rollout under the Future Project implementation phase.

initiatives.title: umontana.ai clearinghouse and 'Be Curious' AI Symposium
initiatives.description: September 2025 launch of umontana.ai as the campus AI clearinghouse. UM's second AI Symposium, 'Be Curious,' was held January 8, 2026, co-hosted by the Provost's Office and IT with tracks on How We Work, How We Teach, and What We Teach (following a first symposium in January 2024).
initiatives.significance: Annual AI symposia are emerging as the signature community-building ritual for mid-size public R1s navigating AI strategy.

investment.funding_source: Office of Strategic Planning & Implementation; Provost; IT; Craton

initiatives.title: Syllabus-level GenAI policy requirement + updated Student Conduct Code
initiatives.description: All UM instructors are required to include a GenAI statement in every syllabus. The Mansfield Library integrated AI literacy into its information-literacy curriculum in November 2024, and the Student Code of Conduct was updated to address generative AI in its definition of plagiarism.
initiatives.significance: Classic example of policy-at-the-course-level as an interim governance pattern while enterprise policy matures.
initiatives.policy_implications: Decentralizes policy to course level while preserving an institution-wide floor (plagiarism code covers undisclosed AI).

initiatives.title: AI Data Privacy & FERPA Guidance (Internal Audit)
initiatives.description: UM's Internal Audit office published explicit data-privacy guidance forbidding the input of FERPA-protected student records, HR records, health information, or human-subject research data into unapproved AI tools, and noting the absence of vendor agreements covering security and privacy at the time of publication.
initiatives.significance: Signals that mid-size public R1s are codifying concrete 'do-not-input' lists even in the absence of full enterprise policy.
initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes a FERPA/HIPAA-aware floor even before enterprise AI contracts are signed.

initiatives.title: CIO 100 Award recognition of the Future Project
initiatives.description: UM's signature AI initiative, the Future Project, earned a 2026 CIO 100 Award recognizing excellence in enterprise-scale technology leadership and innovation. CIO Zach Rossmiller led the award-winning initiative.
initiatives.significance: External validation that a deliberative, human-centered strategy can compete with rapid-adoption narratives.

initiatives.title: Alignment with Montana Executive Order 5-2025 (406 JOBS Initiative)
initiatives.description: Governor Gianforte's August 2025 EO 5-2025 creates the 406 JOBS Initiative, directing Department of Labor, OPI, the Office of the Commissioner of Higher Education, and MUS to expand AI-skilling opportunities and to provide professional development for educators to incorporate AI skills into career-readiness instruction.
initiatives.significance: Aligns UM workforce-facing AI programming with state policy and federal workforce goals.

investment.funding_source: State of Montana / Department of Labor and Industry
investment.description: Cross-agency workforce-development initiative; specific MUS allocation not disclosed.

partners: Montana Department of Labor and Industry
Office of Commissioner of Higher Education

initiatives.policy_implications: UM's workforce-aligned AI programming gains a state-policy anchor.

research_centers.name: Center for Human Centered AI (planned / approved by Board of Regents Nov 2025)
research_centers.focus: Interdisciplinary coordination across education, business, computer science, humanities, health, and creative arts

research_centers.name: Autonomous Aerial Systems Office (AASO)
research_centers.focus: UAV/atmospheric science with AI-accelerated weather forecasting on Oracle Cloud

research_centers.name: SMART FIRES AI/ML team (NSF EPSCoR RII Track-1, UM role)
research_centers.focus: AI/ML decision-support tools for prescribed-fire and wildfire management

ai_academic_programs.name: UM-designed course on AI for ethical leadership (OOLD)
Artificial Intelligence in the Classroom: Faculty Inquiry Project

computing_infrastructure.summary: UM does not operate a named on-campus AI supercomputer. Research computing is hybrid: legacy in-house HPC for some workloads, plus Oracle Cloud Infrastructure HPC (OCI) accessed via the Oracle for Research program for AI-accelerated weather modeling. Statewide HPC leadership sits at Montana State University (Tempest cluster); UM researchers also access MSU and tribal-college resources under SMART FIRES.

investments_and_gifts.title: Craton philanthropic support for the Future Project
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Launch and sustain UM's Future Project AI strategy initiative

investments_and_gifts.title: UM Flagship Fund 2024 AI track
investments_and_gifts.purpose: One-year grants (up to $10K each) to build generative-AI capacity

investments_and_gifts.title: SMART FIRES NSF EPSCoR RII Track-1 award (UM partner)
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Five-year statewide AI/ML + fire-science research and workforce initiative

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Philanthropic advisory partner launching the Future Project
OCI HPC for AI-accelerated weather forecasting
ChatGPT EDU enterprise access and OpenAI Academy training pilot
Claude model access through Amplify and ChatMT

industry_and_research_partnerships.partner: Montana State University, Montana Tech, Salish Kootenai College, Little Big Horn College, Flathead Valley CC
industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: SMART FIRES statewide consortium with tribal and community colleges
ChatMT MUS-wide deployment operated by UM

student_facing_ai.name: Amplify GenAI (chat.umontana.ai)
student_facing_ai.scope: Campus-wide multi-model chat for students, faculty, staff

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Equitable AI access via Amplify GenAI and ChatMT
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UM cites 'equitable access to quality AI tools for the campus community' as a core rationale for Amplify GenAI and MUS-wide ChatMT, explicitly reducing reliance on premium personal subscriptions.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Accessibility guidance against mandatory paid AI tools
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UM's teaching guidance recommends avoiding assignments that require students to purchase premium AI subscriptions and flags equity concerns with AI-detection tools (disproportionate false positives for multilingual and neurodivergent writers).

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Tribal college participation in SMART FIRES AI/ML workforce
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: SMART FIRES explicitly includes Salish Kootenai College and Little Big Horn College as partners in AI/ML workforce development, embedding Native American institutions in the AI training pipeline.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Mansfield Library AI literacy in information-literacy curriculum
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: In November 2024 Mansfield Library librarians revised the information-literacy curriculum to integrate AI literacy, emphasizing accessible and inclusive instruction.

regulatory_context.summary: Montana has notable state-level AI policy posture. Governor Gianforte signed Executive Order 5-2025 (August 2025) creating the 406 JOBS Initiative directing MUS, OPI, and OCHE to expand AI-workforce skilling. Montana has also been recognized as the first state to guarantee 'computational freedom' (HB 178, 2025 session). At the system level, the MUS AI Task Force reviews campus AI approaches, and the Board of Regents approved preliminary AI-institute plans from both UM and MSU in November 2025. A Trump administration federal review of state AI laws has focused on Montana.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: Early-2023 media coverage flagged UM's 'wild west' approach to ChatGPT with no campus-wide rules, leaving decisions to individual professors. UM subsequently updated the Student Code of Conduct plagiarism definition to include generative AI and issued faculty syllabus-statement guidance.

narrative_summary:
The University of Montana, a public R1 in Missoula with roughly 11,000 students, has emerged as a notable mid-size example of deliberate, values-first AI adoption. Its signature effort is the Future Project, a three-phase university-wide strategy launched in spring 2025 with philanthropic backing from Montana-based consultancy Craton, organized around four interlocking lines (How We Teach, What We Teach, How We Work, Industry Demands) and coordinated by more than 55 faculty and staff. President Seth Bodnar's 2025 statement anchors UM's approach in 'integrity, urgency, and care' and in academic values — critical inquiry, transparency, human dignity, and equitable access — explicitly positioning UM against rapid-adoption narratives. The effort earned a 2026 CIO 100 Award and is converging on a planned Center for Human Centered AI, approved in preliminary form by the Montana Board of Regents in November 2025, that will span education, business, computer science, humanities, health, and creative arts.

Operationally, UM is unusually active for a non-elite R1. It runs Amplify GenAI (chat.umontana.ai) as a multi-model gateway, launched ChatGPT EDU campus-wide with an OpenAI Academy training cohort in May 2025, and in May 2026 brings ChatMT — a multi-model AWS Bedrock / LibreChat platform covering the entire Montana University System — into production under UM's stewardship. Research AI is anchored by the $20M NSF EPSCoR SMART FIRES award, where UM is the lead environmental-science partner alongside MSU, Montana Tech, two tribal colleges, and a community college, and by an Oracle Cloud partnership that accelerates WRF weather-model runs by 60%.

Policy is intentionally permissive but not absent: every syllabus must include a GenAI statement, the Student Conduct Code has been updated for AI-assisted plagiarism, Internal Audit publishes explicit FERPA/HR/health/human-subject 'do-not-input' rules, and faculty are directed not to use AI detection tools. Gaps include the absence of a dedicated chief AI officer (the three-person Future Project lead team fills the role), no disclosed dollar amount for Craton's gift, limited public detail on on-campus GPU infrastructure, and no formal AI degree or certificate program — those sit at sister MSU, not UM. Montana's aggressive state context (EO 5-2025 '406 JOBS', first-in-nation 'computational freedom' law, and a federal review of state AI laws) frames UM's approach as both politically visible and closely watched.

gaps_acknowledged: Exact dollar amount of Craton's gift for the Future Project is not publicly disclosed
No confirmed AI degree, minor, or certificate at UM (such programs sit primarily at Montana State); possible smaller unit-level offerings may exist
No named AI-specific on-campus HPC cluster or GPU count disclosed on UM sites; research computing is hybrid cloud + MSU partnership
Identity of UM's interim/new president following Seth Bodnar's Jan 2026 step-down is not yet named in public sources reviewed
Student-facing retention or admissions predictive-analytics tooling (separate from GenAI chat) is not clearly documented in public sources
No known AI-related litigation or formal FERPA enforcement case against UM
Exact user counts for Amplify GenAI pilot beyond the initial 75 and for ChatMT at launch are not public

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: platform first.
